description: Add 17 new browser tools to the OpenTabs platform, expanding from 6 basic tab management tools to a full browser automation toolkit. Covers tab content extraction, screenshots, DOM interaction (click, type, select, wait, query), cookie management, network request capture, and console log capture. Each tool follows the existing browser tool patterns (MCP server definition + extension handler + message routing).

userStories.title: Add manifest permissions and interactive test page
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the Chrome extension manifest to include cookies and debugger permissions, and the E2E test server to serve an interactive page, so that new browser tools have the permissions they need and E2E tests have a page with interactive elements to test against.
acceptanceCriteria: manifest.json permissions array includes 'cookies' and 'debugger'
e2e/test-server.ts serves GET /interactive that returns an HTML page with: a
The interactive page has <title>Interactive Test Page</title>
BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES in e2e/helpers.ts is updated to include all 23 browser tool names (existing 6 + 17 new)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:
1. platform/browser-extension/manifest.json — add 'cookies' and 'debugger' to the permissions array (line 13). Keep existing permissions.
2. e2e/test-server.ts — add a new route handler for GET /interactive. Follow the existing pattern for how the test server serves HTML. The page should be a self-contained HTML document with inline <script> for the button click handler and delayed content. Use the existing Bun.serve() fetch handler pattern.
3. e2e/helpers.ts — update the BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES array (line 206) to include all new tool names: browser_focus_tab, browser_get_tab_info, browser_screenshot_tab, browser_get_tab_content, browser_click_element, browser_type_text, browser_select_option, browser_wait_for_element, browser_query_elements, browser_get_cookies, browser_set_cookie, browser_delete_cookies, browser_enable_network_capture, browser_get_network_requests, browser_disable_network_capture, browser_get_console_logs, browser_clear_console_logs.

The interactive page HTML should be minimal but functional. No external dependencies. Inline CSS is fine. The delayed content div should start with style='display:none' and be shown after 500ms.

userStories.title: Add browser_focus_tab and browser_get_tab_info tools
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to focus a specific browser tab and get detailed information about a tab, so that I can activate tabs before screenshotting and inspect tab metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_focus_tab tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/focus-tab.ts
browser_get_tab_info tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-tab-info.ts
Both tools are exported from platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/index.ts barrel
Extension handlers handleBrowserFocusTab and handleBrowserGetTabInfo exist in browser-commands.ts
Message router dispatch table has entries for 'browser.focusTab' and 'browser.getTabInfo'
browser_focus_tab accepts { tabId: number } and returns { id, title, url, active: true }
browser_get_tab_info accepts { tabId: number } and returns { id, title, url, status, active, windowId, favIconUrl, incognito }
browser_focus_tab calls chrome.tabs.update(tabId, { active: true }) then chrome.windows.update(tab.windowId, { focused: true })
browser_get_tab_info calls chrome.tabs.get(tabId)
Both tools handle errors (invalid tabId) with proper JSON-RPC error responses
Zod descriptions are accurate and informational
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Follow the exact pattern of existing browser tools:

MCP server side (platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/):
- focus-tab.ts: import { defineBrowserTool } from './definition.js'; import { dispatchToExtension } from '../extension-protocol.js'; import { z } from 'zod'; Use dispatchToExtension(state, 'browser.focusTab', { tabId: args.tabId }).
- get-tab-info.ts: Same pattern, dispatch 'browser.getTabInfo'.
- Add both to index.ts barrel array.

Extension side (platform/browser-extension/src/):
- browser-commands.ts: Add handleBrowserFocusTab and handleBrowserGetTabInfo. Follow the exact pattern of handleBrowserOpenTab — validate params, call chrome API, sendToServer with result or error. For focusTab: const tab = await chrome.tabs.update(tabId, { active: true }); await chrome.windows.update(tab.windowId, { focused: true }); Return { id: tab.id, title: tab.title, url: tab.url, active: true }.
- message-router.ts: Add entries to methodHandlers Map, following the exact pattern of existing browser.* entries.

Zod descriptions:
- focus-tab: description = 'Focus a browser tab by making it the active tab in its window and bringing the window to the foreground. Required before using browser_screenshot_tab. Use browser_list_tabs to find tab IDs.'
- get-tab-info: description = 'Get detailed information about a specific browser tab including loading status, URL, title, favicon URL, and whether it is active or incognito. Use browser_list_tabs to find tab IDs.'

userStories.title: Add browser_get_tab_content tool
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to extract text content from a web page, so that I can understand what a page contains without writing custom JavaScript every time.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_get_tab_content tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-tab-content.ts
Tool is exported from the browser-tools barrel
Extension handler handleBrowserGetTabContent exists in browser-commands.ts
Message router has entry for 'browser.getTabContent'
Tool accepts { tabId: number, selector?: string, maxLength?: number }
Tool returns { title: string, url: string, content: string }
Uses chrome.scripting.executeScript with { func } parameter to extract text
Default selector is 'body', default maxLength is 50000
Content is trimmed and truncated to maxLength
Error handling for invalid tabId and non-existent selector
Zod descriptions are accurate
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
MCP server tool (get-tab-content.ts):
- Zod input: z.object({ tabId: z.number().int().positive().describe('Tab ID to extract content from'), selector: z.string().optional().describe('CSS selector to scope extraction — defaults to body'), maxLength: z.number().int().positive().optional().describe('Maximum characters to return — defaults to 50000, increase for long pages') })
- Description: 'Extract the visible text content of a web page or a specific element. Returns the page title, current URL, and text content. Use the selector parameter to scope extraction to a specific section. Useful for understanding page content without writing custom JavaScript.'
- Dispatch: dispatchToExtension(state, 'browser.getTabContent', { tabId, selector: args.selector ?? 'body', maxLength: args.maxLength ?? 50000 })

Extension handler (browser-commands.ts):
- handleBrowserGetTabContent(params, id):
  1. Validate tabId, selector, maxLength from params
  2. Use chrome.scripting.executeScript({ target: { tabId }, world: 'MAIN', func: (sel, max) => { const el = document.querySelector(sel); if (!el) return { error: 'Element not found: ' + sel }; return { title: document.title, url: document.URL, content: (el.innerText || '').trim().slice(0, max) }; }, args: [selector, maxLength] })
  3. Read result from results[0].result
  4. If error property, send JSON-RPC error
  5. Otherwise sendToServer with result

Note: Using { func } (not file-based injection) because the code is static and known at build time. This works regardless of page CSP because chrome.scripting.executeScript with func references is not subject to CSP restrictions.

All tools handle element-not-found errors gracefully

wait_for_element accepts { tabId, selector, timeout?, visible? } and polls until element found
wait_for_element returns { found: true, tagName, text } or error on timeout
Default timeout is 10000ms, default visible is false
query_elements accepts { tabId, selector, limit?, attributes? } and returns matching elements
query_elements returns { count, elements: Array<{ tagName, text, attributes }> }
Default limit is 100, default attributes are id, class, href, src, type, name, value, placeholder
Both use chrome.scripting.executeScript with { func }

userStories.title: Add cookie tools: browser_get_cookies, browser_set_cookie, browser_delete_cookies
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to read, set, and delete browser cookies, so that I can inspect authentication state, debug session issues, and manage cookie-based configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_get_cookies tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-cookies.ts
browser_set_cookie tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/set-cookie.ts
browser_delete_cookies tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/delete-cookies.ts
All three exported from browser-tools barrel
Extension handlers exist in browser-commands.ts
Message router has entries for browser.getCookies, browser.setCookie, browser.deleteCookies
get_cookies accepts { url, name? } using safeUrl validation, returns { cookies: Array<cookie> }
set_cookie accepts { url, name, value, domain?, path?, secure?, httpOnly?, expirationDate? }
delete_cookies accepts { url, name } and returns { deleted: true }
URL parameters use the safeUrl schema from url-validation.ts (rejects javascript:, data:, etc.)
Extension handler uses chrome.cookies.getAll, chrome.cookies.set, chrome.cookies.remove
Zod descriptions are accurate
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
These tools use chrome.cookies API, which requires the 'cookies' permission (added in US-001).

get-cookies.ts:
- Import safeUrl from './url-validation.js'
- Input: { url: safeUrl.describe('URL to get cookies for — returns all cookies that would be sent with a request to this URL'), name: z.string().optional().describe('Filter by cookie name — omit to get all cookies for the URL') }
- Description: 'Get cookies for a URL. Returns all cookies that would be sent with a request to that URL, including HttpOnly cookies not accessible to JavaScript. Optionally filter by cookie name.'
- Dispatch: 'browser.getCookies'
- Extension handler: const cookies = await chrome.cookies.getAll({ url }); if (name) filter by name. Map to { name, value, domain, path, secure, httpOnly, sameSite, expirationDate }.

set-cookie.ts:
- Input: { url: safeUrl.describe('URL to associate the cookie with'), name: z.string().min(1).describe('Cookie name'), value: z.string().describe('Cookie value'), domain: z.string().optional(), path: z.string().optional(), secure: z.boolean().optional(), httpOnly: z.boolean().optional(), expirationDate: z.number().optional().describe('Unix timestamp for cookie expiry — omit for session cookie') }
- Description: 'Set a browser cookie. Creates a new cookie or overwrites an existing one with the same name, domain, and path.'
- Extension handler: const cookie = await chrome.cookies.set({ url, name, value, ...optional fields }); Return the cookie object.

delete-cookies.ts:
- Input: { url: safeUrl.describe('URL of the cookie to delete'), name: z.string().min(1).describe('Name of the cookie to delete') }
- Description: 'Delete a specific browser cookie by URL and name.'
- Extension handler: await chrome.cookies.remove({ url, name }); Return { deleted: true, name, url }.

userStories.title: Add network capture state management in extension
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the browser extension to manage per-tab network capture buffers using the Chrome DevTools Protocol debugger API, so that network capture tools can start/stop/read request data.
acceptanceCriteria: A new file platform/browser-extension/src/network-capture.ts exists with network capture state management
Exports: startCapture(tabId, maxRequests, urlFilter?), stopCapture(tabId), getRequests(tabId, clear?), isCapturing(tabId)
Per-tab state stored in a module-level Map<number, CaptureState>
startCapture attaches chrome.debugger({tabId}, '1.3'), enables Network.enable and Runtime.enable
Listens for Network.requestWillBeSent, Network.responseReceived events via chrome.debugger.onEvent
Captured requests stored as { url, method, status?, statusText?, requestHeaders?, responseHeaders?, mimeType?, timing? }
Buffer respects maxRequests limit (drops oldest when full)
urlFilter filters requests by URL substring match
stopCapture detaches debugger and clears the buffer
getRequests returns the buffer contents, optionally clearing it
isCapturing returns whether capture is active for a tab
Console logs from Runtime.consoleAPICalled are captured in a separate buffer per tab
Exports: getConsoleLogs(tabId, clear?, level?), clearConsoleLogs(tabId)
chrome.tabs.onRemoved listener cleans up capture state for closed tabs
TypeScript types are clean, no 'as any' casts
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes

userStories.title: Add network capture MCP tools: enable, get, disable
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to capture and inspect network requests made by a browser tab, so that I can debug API calls, inspect request/response data, and understand web app behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_enable_network_capture tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/enable-network-capture.ts
browser_get_network_requests tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-network-requests.ts
browser_disable_network_capture tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/disable-network-capture.ts
All three exported from browser-tools barrel
Extension handlers exist in browser-commands.ts that call functions from network-capture.ts
Message router has entries for browser.enableNetworkCapture, browser.getNetworkRequests, browser.disableNetworkCapture
enable accepts { tabId, maxRequests?, urlFilter? } and returns { enabled: true, tabId }
get accepts { tabId, clear? } and returns { requests: Array<captured request> }
disable accepts { tabId } and returns { disabled: true, tabId }
Zod descriptions are accurate and informational
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
These MCP tools dispatch to the extension, which delegates to network-capture.ts (US-008).

enable-network-capture.ts:
- Input: { tabId: z.number().int().positive().describe('Tab ID to capture network requests for'), maxRequests: z.number().int().positive().optional().describe('Maximum requests to buffer before dropping oldest — defaults to 100'), urlFilter: z.string().optional().describe('Only capture requests whose URL contains this substring') }
- Description: 'Start capturing network requests and responses for a browser tab using the Chrome DevTools Protocol. Captures request URL, method, status, headers, and timing. Retrieve captured data with browser_get_network_requests. Only one capture session per tab — call browser_disable_network_capture first to restart.'
- Dispatch: 'browser.enableNetworkCapture'

get-network-requests.ts:
- Input: { tabId: z.number().int().positive().describe('Tab ID to get captured requests for'), clear: z.boolean().optional().describe('Clear the request buffer after reading — defaults to false') }
- Description: 'Get network requests captured since browser_enable_network_capture was called on this tab. Returns URL, HTTP method, status code, request/response headers, MIME type, and timing for each request.'
- Dispatch: 'browser.getNetworkRequests'

disable-network-capture.ts:
- Input: { tabId: z.number().int().positive().describe('Tab ID to stop capturing for') }
- Description: 'Stop capturing network requests for a tab and release the Chrome DevTools Protocol debugger. Clears the request buffer.'
- Dispatch: 'browser.disableNetworkCapture'

Extension handlers in browser-commands.ts:
- handleBrowserEnableNetworkCapture: import { startCapture } from './network-capture.js'; validate tabId; await startCapture(tabId, maxRequests, urlFilter); sendToServer result.
- handleBrowserGetNetworkRequests: import { getRequests } from './network-capture.js'; validate tabId; const requests = getRequests(tabId, clear); sendToServer.
- handleBrowserDisableNetworkCapture: import { stopCapture } from './network-capture.js'; stopCapture(tabId); sendToServer.

userStories.title: Add browser_get_console_logs and browser_clear_console_logs tools
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to read console log output from browser tabs, so that I can debug JavaScript errors and understand application behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: browser_get_console_logs tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/get-console-logs.ts
browser_clear_console_logs tool exists at platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/clear-console-logs.ts
Both tools exported from browser-tools barrel
Extension handlers exist in browser-commands.ts
Message router has entries for browser.getConsoleLogs and browser.clearConsoleLogs
get_console_logs accepts { tabId, clear?, level? } and returns { logs: Array<{ level, message, timestamp }> }
clear_console_logs accepts { tabId } and returns { cleared: true }
Console logs are captured when network capture is active (debugger attached with Runtime.enable)
Level filter accepts 'all', 'log', 'warn', 'error', 'info', 'debug'
Zod descriptions are accurate
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
userStories.notes:
Console logs are captured by the debugger's Runtime.consoleAPICalled events, which are already set up in network-capture.ts (US-008). These MCP tools just read and clear the buffer.

get-console-logs.ts:
- Input: { tabId: z.number().int().positive().describe('Tab ID to get console logs from — network capture must be enabled first via browser_enable_network_capture'), clear: z.boolean().optional().describe('Clear the log buffer after reading — defaults to false'), level: z.enum(['all', 'log', 'warn', 'error', 'info', 'debug']).optional().describe('Filter by log level — defaults to all') }
- Description: 'Get console log messages from a browser tab. Requires browser_enable_network_capture to be active on the tab (the debugger captures both network requests and console output). Filter by level to see only errors, warnings, etc.'

clear-console-logs.ts:
- Input: { tabId: z.number().int().positive().describe('Tab ID to clear console logs for') }
- Description: 'Clear the console log buffer for a browser tab without disabling capture.'

Extension handlers:
- handleBrowserGetConsoleLogs: import { getConsoleLogs } from './network-capture.js'; validate tabId; const logs = getConsoleLogs(tabId, clear, level); sendToServer.
- handleBrowserClearConsoleLogs: import { clearConsoleLogs } from './network-capture.js'; clearConsoleLogs(tabId); sendToServer({ cleared: true }).

userStories.title: E2E tests for tab management and content tools
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want comprehensive E2E tests for browser_focus_tab, browser_get_tab_info, browser_screenshot_tab, and browser_get_tab_content, so that these tools are verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests exist in e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts
Test: all new browser tools appear in tools/list
Test: browser_focus_tab activates a tab (verify via browser_list_tabs that active=true)
Test: browser_focus_tab with invalid tabId returns error
Test: browser_get_tab_info returns correct fields for an open tab
Test: browser_get_tab_info with invalid tabId returns error
Test: browser_screenshot_tab captures a base64 PNG (verify starts with iVBOR which is the base64 prefix for PNG)
Test: browser_get_tab_content returns title and content from test server page
Test: browser_get_tab_content with CSS selector scopes extraction
Test: browser_get_tab_content with non-existent selector returns error
All tests use the existing E2E fixture pattern (mcpServer, extensionContext, mcpClient, testServer)
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Add new test.describe blocks to e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts following the existing test patterns.

Tool listing test: Update the existing 'browser tools appear in tools/list' test to also check for all new tool names.

browser_focus_tab tests:
- Open a second tab via browser_open_tab, then focus it. Verify via browser_list_tabs that the focused tab has active=true.
- Call with tabId=999999, expect isError=true.

browser_get_tab_info tests:
- Open a tab to the test server, get its info. Verify id, title, url, status fields exist and have correct types.
- Call with tabId=999999, expect isError=true.

browser_screenshot_tab tests:
- Open test server tab, call browser_screenshot_tab. Parse result, verify image field is a string, verify it starts with 'iVBOR' (base64 PNG magic bytes).
- Note: The test may need a small delay after opening the tab for the page to render.

browser_get_tab_content tests:
- Open test server tab, call browser_get_tab_content. Verify title matches test server's page title. Verify content is a non-empty string.
- Call with selector='h1' (if test server page has one) or a known element.
- Call with selector='#nonexistent' and verify error response.

Use the existing openTestServerTab helper for tab setup. Clean up tabs after each test.

userStories.notes:
Cookie lifecycle test:
1. Set a cookie: callTool('browser_set_cookie', { url: testServer.url, name: '__opentabs_e2e', value: 'test123' })
2. Get cookies: callTool('browser_get_cookies', { url: testServer.url, name: '__opentabs_e2e' })
3. Verify the cookie appears with correct name/value
4. Delete: callTool('browser_delete_cookies', { url: testServer.url, name: '__opentabs_e2e' })
5. Get again and verify it's gone

URL validation tests: Follow the exact pattern of the existing 'Browser tools — URL validation' test.describe block. Test that javascript:, data:, and file: URLs are rejected by all three cookie tools.

Note: Cookie tests need the extension context fixture (for chrome.cookies API access). Use the same test fixture pattern as other browser tool tests.

userStories.title: E2E tests for network capture and console log tools
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests for the network capture lifecycle and console log tools, so that these advanced debugging tools are verified end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests exist in e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts
Test: enable network capture, navigate tab to make a request, get network requests, verify at least one request captured
Test: disable network capture, verify capture stops
Test: get_network_requests with clear=true empties the buffer
Test: enable with urlFilter only captures matching requests
Test: get_console_logs returns logs after enabling capture and executing console.log via execute_script
Test: clear_console_logs empties the log buffer
Test: enable network capture on non-existent tab returns error
bun run build passes
bun run type-check passes
bun run lint passes
bun run knip passes
bun run test passes
bun run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Network capture lifecycle test:
1. Open test server tab
2. Enable capture: callTool('browser_enable_network_capture', { tabId })
3. Navigate tab to a different URL to trigger network requests: callTool('browser_navigate_tab', { tabId, url: testServer.url + '/interactive' })
4. Wait briefly for requests to be captured (500ms)
5. Get requests: callTool('browser_get_network_requests', { tabId })
6. Verify requests array has at least 1 entry with url, method, status fields
7. Disable capture: callTool('browser_disable_network_capture', { tabId })

Clear test:
1. After enabling + navigating, get with clear=true
2. Get again, verify empty

urlFilter test:
1. Enable with urlFilter: '/interactive'
2. Navigate to /interactive
3. Get requests, verify all URLs contain '/interactive'

Console test:
1. Open tab, enable network capture (which also enables Runtime)
2. Execute: callTool('browser_execute_script', { tabId, code: 'console.log("test-message"); console.error("test-error")' })
3. Wait 500ms
4. Get logs: callTool('browser_get_console_logs', { tabId })
5. Verify logs array contains entries with message matching 'test-message' and 'test-error'
6. Clear logs: callTool('browser_clear_console_logs', { tabId })
7. Get again, verify empty

Note: The debugger permission shows a yellow warning banner in Chrome. In headless mode this shouldn't block tests.
